name: Caribbean Red Snapper
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the red snapper with salt and pepper, both inside and out.
2. Rub the fish with lime quarters.
3. Sprinkle the fish evenly with flour.
4. Combine rice, shrimp, green onions, celery, and ginger root in a bowl.
5. Stuff the mixture into the fish cavity.
6. Close the fish by skewering or sewing the opening.
7. Place the fish in a heavily buttered baking pan.
8. Score the top of the fish in an attractive design to prevent it from buckling during cooking.
9. Lay bacon slices over the top of the fish.
10. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes, or until the fish flakes easily with a fork.
11. Transfer the fish to a warm platter.
12. Pour the white wine into the baking pan and deglaze it by scraping up any brown bits from the bottom of the pan.
13. Pour the liquid over the fish.
